_note: 现任主帅;换帅经 --set 更新(旧任压进 history,只增不改)。下游:match_header 主帅行。
brief:
- Darren Bazeley, English ex-defender (Watford/Wolves/Walsall), became New Zealand head coach in 2023 after working at NZ FA since 2009 in age-group roles, succeeding former Walsall teammate Danny Hay.
- Coaching since 2011, Bazeley leads the All Whites to the June 11-July 19 finals after New Zealand missed three consecutive World Cups (2014, 2018, 2022).
- Tactical profiles conflict: one source describes a 4-2-3-1 built on physical defending, set-piece threat, and feeding striker Chris Wood; another cites a modern possession-based approach that stretches opponents wide.
- The team references their 2010 World Cup campaign, where they exited unbeaten after drawing with Slovakia, Italy, and Paraguay.
- Creation remains a weakness: against organized blocks like Iran, New Zealand struggles to generate open-play chances, heightening reliance on dead balls and Wood's finishing.
- Bazeley's squad features 34-year-old striker Chris Wood, who returned from December knee surgery to anchor the attack, embodying the side's physical, direct identity.
